name: code-review-assistant
description: Comprehensive PR review using multi-agent swarm with specialized reviewers for security, performance, style, tests, and documentation. Provides detailed feedback with auto-fix suggestions and merge readiness assessment.
capabilities: Coordinates 5 specialized AI agents for parallel code review
Analyzes security vulnerabilities, performance bottlenecks, code style, test coverage, and documentation
Posts review comments directly to GitHub pull requests
Generates automatic fix suggestions using Codex reasoning
Calculates overall code quality scores and merge readiness assessment
Approves or requests changes based on quality gates
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Legitimate code review automation skill. Uses GitHub CLI and claude-flow tools to coordinate multi-agent PR reviews. All filesystem operations scoped to temporary review directories. No credential theft, data exfiltration, or persistence mechanisms detected. Static findings are false positives from scanner misinterpreting documentation patterns and metadata.
valueStatement: Manual code reviews are slow and inconsistent. The Code Review Assistant uses a multi-agent swarm to analyze pull requests across security, performance, style, tests, and documentation in parallel, then posts detailed feedback with fix suggestions directly to GitHub.
userTitle: Review pull requests with AI agents
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI authentication and repository access
Relies on claude-flow tools being available in the environment
Does not execute tests or build code locally

useCases.title: Automated PR feedback
useCases.description: Get instant, comprehensive feedback on every pull request without waiting for human reviewers.

useCases.title: Quality gates for merge
useCases.description: Enforce code quality standards automatically before merging pull requests to main branches.

useCases.title: Consistent review standards
useCases.description: Ensure all team members receive the same thorough analysis across security, performance, and style.

promptTemplates.title: Security focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Review PR {number} focusing on security vulnerabilities only

promptTemplates.title: Quick quality check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run code-review-assistant {number} with security,performance,tests suggestions enabled

promptTemplates.title: Full audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Review PR {number} with all focus areas and auto-merge if score is above 90

bestPractices: Run code reviews early in the development cycle to catch issues before they accumulate
Combine automated reviews with human expertise for best results
Address security findings immediately regardless of overall score
antiPatterns: Ignoring automated reviews and merging without addressing findings
Relying solely on automated reviews without human oversight
Using code review as a gatekeeper rather than a learning opportunity

faq.question: What AI tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for multi-agent coordination.

faq.question: What GitHub permissions are needed?
faq.answer: Requires GitHub CLI authentication with repo read/write access for PR comments.

faq.question: How long does a full review take?
faq.answer: Typical reviews complete in 2-5 minutes depending on PR size and focus areas.

faq.question: Is my code data sent externally?
faq.answer: Code stays within your environment. Only review results are posted to GitHub.

faq.question: What if the review finds critical issues?
faq.answer: Critical security issues block merge and generate detailed fix suggestions immediately.

faq.question: How does this compare to GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Provides deeper analysis with AI agents rather than static rule-based checks.
